quotes: Sin that his lord was twenty year of age.

records.ety: [OE. sinne, AS. synn, syn; akin to D. zonde, OS. sundia, OHG. sunta, G. sünde, Icel., Dan. & Sw. synd, L. sons, sontis, guilty, perhaps originally from the p. pr. of the verb signifying, to be, and meaning, the one who it is. Cf. Authentic, Sooth.]

defs: Transgression of the law of God; disobedience of the divine command; any violation of God's will, either in purpose or conduct; moral deficiency in the character; iniquity; as, sins of omission and sins of commission.
quotes: Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.
Sin is the transgression of the law.
I think 't no sin. To cozen him that would unjustly win.
Enthralled By sin to foul, exorbitant desires.

defs: An offense, in general; a violation of propriety; a misdemeanor; as, a sin against good manners.
quotes: I grant that poetry's a crying sin.

defs: A sin offering; a sacrifice for sin.
quotes: He hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin.

defs: An embodiment of sin; a very wicked person.
quotes: Thy ambition, Thou scarlet sin, robbed this bewailing land Of noble Buckingham.
notes: ☞ Sin is used in the formation of some compound words of obvious signification; as, sin-born; sin-bred, sin-oppressed, sin-polluted, and the like.

records.ety: [OE. sinnen, singen, sinegen, AS. syngian. See Sin, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sinned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sinning.]

defs: To depart voluntarily from the path of duty prescribed by God to man; to violate the divine law in any particular, by actual transgression or by the neglect or nonobservance of its injunctions; to violate any known rule of duty; -- often followed by against.
quotes: Against thee, thee only, have I sinned.
All have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.

defs: To violate human rights, law, or propriety; to commit an offense; to trespass; to transgress.
quotes: I am a man More sinned against than sinning.
Who but wishes to invert the laws Of order, sins against the eternal cause.
